input.user_input: How does this lease agreement define 'tenant default' and what remedies does the landlord have?

actual:
You have not provided or uploaded a lease agreement for me to review. 

Please upload the document, and I will gladly analyze it to explain exactly how it defines a "tenant default" and what specific remedies it grants to the landlord. 

In general, lease agreements typically define a tenant default as failing to pay rent on time, violating use restrictions, abandoning the property, or breaching other material lease covenants. Landlord remedies often include the right to terminate the lease, evict the tenant, accelerate future rent, or recover damages and legal fees, but the exact terms will depend entirely on the language in your specific contract.
